text: **Anna:** Hello, I'm Anna.
**Professor Bot:** And I'm Pete.
**Pete:** {Welcome|ようこそ/歓迎} to He Said She Said.
**Pete:** Because there are always two {sides|側面/観点}
**Professor Bot:** To every story, today's show is about {circus|サーカス} arts.
**Pete:** {Circus|サーカス}?
**Pete:** That's not an art {form|形態/様式}.
**Anna:** Yes, it is.
**Pete:** No, it isn't.
**Anna:** Yes, it is.
**Pete:** No, it isn't.
**Anna:** Yes, it is.
**Pete:** No, no, it isn't.
**Anna:** Yes, it is.
**Pete:** No, it isn't
**Anna:** Yes it is.
**Professor Bot:** Oh dear Anna and Pete do not {agree|同意する} about circus arts
**Professor Bot:** But that's okay.
**Anna:** We all have different {opinions|意見}.
**Professor Bot:** Today's lesson is about {agreeing|同意} and {disagreeing|意見の相違}.
**Professor Bot:** Let's listen to how Anna and Pete {disagree|意見が合わない} or {agree|同意する}.
**Professor Bot:** Let's hope these two can {agree|同意する} on something.
**Anna:** Today, let's run away with the {circus|サーカス}.
**Anna:** Recently, I went to a {circus|サーカス} {festival|祭典}.
**Anna:** There was a huge {circus|サーカス} {tent|テント} and many different circus {performers|実演者}.
**Anna:** It was a {celebration|祝典} of circus arts.
**Pete:** That looks fun, Anna, but I don't think circus {performers|実演者} are {artists|芸術家}.
**Anna:** I think they're {athletes|運動選手} with interesting {skills|技能} and {costumes|衣装}.
**Anna:** Hmm, that is a very interesting {point of view|見方}.
**Anna:** And I completely {disagree|意見が合わない}.
**Anna:** Anna.
**Anna:** {Michelangelo|画家・彫刻家|/ˌmaɪkəlˈændʒəloʊ/|Michelangelo painted the Sistine Chapel.} was an artist.
**Anna:** {Rembrandt|画家|/ˈrɛmbrɑːnt/|Rembrandt painted many portraits.} was an artist.
**Anna:** People who {swing|揺れる/振る|/swɪŋ/|Children swing on the playground.} from ropes are not artists.
**Anna:** I know it's not easy to {swing|揺れる/振る} from a bar and {catch|捕まえる|/kætʃ/|Can you catch the ball?} someone by the hands.
**Anna:** You have to be very {athletic|運動神経の良い|/æθˈletɪk/|Athletes are very athletic.} to do that.
**Anna:** That's a good point, Pete.
**Pete:** They are athletic.
**Pete:** But they are called {trapeze|空中ブランコ|/trəˈpiːz/|Trapeze artists perform high in the air.} artists.
**Pete:** Trapeze artists.
**Pete:** I hear what you're saying, Anna.
**Pete:** I do, but where is the art?
**Pete:** Look at these guys
**Pete:** They're {jugglers|曲芸師|/ˈdʒʌɡlərz/|Jugglers throw many objects in the air.}
**Anna:** Yes, they are skilled in the art of {juggling|曲芸/手品|/ˈdʒʌɡlɪŋ/|Juggling requires great coordination.}
**Pete:** They have a special skill, but are they artists?
**Pete:** No.
**Pete:** They're just throwing things back and forth.
**Anna:** Kind of like you and me, Pete.
**Anna:** We're going back and forth on this issue.
**Pete:** I don't think we'll ever agree on this one.
**Pete:** No.
**Anna:** But we can agree that these young people
**Anna:** Are amazing.
**Pete:** Okay, yeah, we can agree on that.
**Pete:** But still, they are very athletic.
**Pete:** {Flipping|宙返り/回転|/ˈflɪpɪŋ/|Gymnasts are good at flipping.} and throwing each other around.
**Anna:** Yes, I agree with you on that point.
**Anna:** They did it.
**Pete:** They agreed.
**Anna:** Anna says she agrees that the {performers|演者/パフォーマー|/pərˈfɔːrmərz/|Circus performers are amazing.} are {athletic|運動能力の高い|/æθˈletɪk/|Athletes train hard every day.}.
**Anna:** That's a good point, Pete
**Pete:** They are athletic.
**Anna:** I agree with you on that point.
**Anna:** Now look for other ways they agree.
**Anna:** Anna?
**Anna:** Sorry, sorry.
**Anna:** But he's an {artist|芸術家/アーティスト|/ˈɑːrtɪst/|Artists create beautiful works.} too.
**Anna:** He's telling a story with his {sticks|棒/スティック|/stɪks/|Drumsticks make music.} and his {costume|衣装|/ˈkɒstuːm/|Actors wear costumes on stage.}.
**Pete:** What story?
**Anna:** And what costume?
**Anna:** I see your point, Pete.
**Anna:** I really do.
**Anna:** But circus {performers|演者/パフォーマー|/pərˈfɔːrmərz/|Circus performers are skilled.} do more than physical tricks.
**Anna:** Look, look at these {amazing|驚くべき/素晴らしい|/əˈmeɪzɪŋ/|This is an amazing performance.} performers
**Anna:** I will {admit|認める|/ədˈmɪt/|I admit my mistake.}, I like their costumes.
**Anna:** You know, Pete, let's let the performers speak for themselves.
**Anna:** Kate and Piper tell stories while hanging upside down on a {ring|輪/リング|/rɪŋ/|Gymnasts use a ring.}.
**Pete:** Was it hard to {interview|インタビューする|/ˌɪntərˈvjuː/|Reporters interview celebrities.} them upside down?
**Anna:** I interviewed them right side up, Pete.
**Anna:** Kate says circus performing may be athletic, but it's not {competitive|競争の|/kəmˈpetətɪv/|Sports are competitive.}.
**Anna:** With most sports, you compete.
**Kate:** It's an art form because like other sports you're competing.
**Kate:** This is simply performing and having fun.
**Pete:** Okay, Kate made a really good point.
**Anna:** In athletics, there's a lot of {competition|競争|/kəmˈpetɪʃn/|Competition drives improvement.}.
**Pete:** But still.
**Anna:** Here's Piper, Pete.
**Anna:** Please pay attention, Pete.
**Piper:** But I'm Well, when you're up in the air doing circus, you have to {perform|演じる/実行する|/pərˈfɔːrm/|Actors perform on stage.}.

**Anna:** And so we learn to {embody|体現する/演じる|/ɪmˈbɒdi/|Actors embody their characters.} characters and to move {fluidly|滑らかに|/ˈfluːɪdli/|She moves fluidly on stage.} and {gracefully|優雅に|/ˈgreɪsfəli/|Dancers move gracefully.} in artistic ways.
**Anna:** And that is why they are artists.
**Pete:** You're right, Anna.
**Pete:** Oh, you're right.
**Pete:** It's just so beautiful.
**Anna:** I know.
**Anna:** I know, Pete
**Professor Bot:** Good job, you two.
**Professor Bot:** I'm so glad that Anna and Pete finally {agree|同意する|/əˈɡriː/|We agree on this point.}
**Professor Bot:** Pete said, You're right, Anna.
**Professor Bot:** I agree too.
**Professor Bot:** Circus arts are {beautiful|美しい|/ˈbjuːtɪfəl/|The sunset was beautiful.}
**Professor Bot:** Remember the website.
